name: Sanjay Mandru
work: Integrated Autoware with ROS2-based distributed systems and deployed autonomous workflows on a Lincoln MKZ platform
current: Software Engineer II at Microsoft working on distributed systems
impact: CAVAS experience helped me understand real-world system integration and improved my problem-solving during interviews

name: Anirudh Jayanthi
affiliation: PhD student at UB's autonomous driving research lab (CAVAS)
work: Worked on NS3 network sim component of a 5-in-1 connected autonomous vehicle simulator (2017-2018); worked on GNSS/RTK, ROS and autoware middleware of autonomous Lincoln MKZ (2018-2021); and integrated smart infrastructure and cooperative perception systems (2019-2023)
current: Assistant Professor at Wayne State University
impact: Provided hands-on experience with perception algorithms and real-time software integration within a full autonomous driving stack, demonstrating how to translate research into production-oriented software.

name: Daniel Vadranapu
work: Worked extensively on the Lincoln platform, contributing to real-world AV system development and co-authored a research paper with Professor Chunming Qiao
current: Robotics Software Engineer at General Motors
impact: Helped build a strong understanding of end-to-end autonomous vehicle systems and provided real-world context that improved the ability to discuss practical systems during interviews.

name: Foad Hajiaghajani Memar (Foad)
work: Studied the security and robustness of perception systems in autonomous vehicles
current: Software Engineer at iDrive Inc. focusing on building world models for L2+ autonomous driving systems
impact: Significantly strengthened fundamentals in AV security and helped secure interview opportunities, ultimately leading to a full-time role at iDrive Inc.

name: Jakye Amos
affiliation: High School Student at City Honors
work: Worked on Olli autonomous shuttle integration
current: Graduating senior Computer Science major from Case Western Reserve University and 3x Amazon intern
impact: Being part of something so complex and rigorous at a young age allowed me to understand what it took to be a cog in something much bigger than myself.

name: DeeKay Goswami
work: Worked on integrating Autoware (especially Drive-By-Wire) on Lincoln with all ROS2 nodes
current: AD/ADAS Perception Software Engineer at LUCID MOTORS INC working on computer vision and localization problems
impact: Hands-on learning on the Lincoln platform exposed real-world complexities that simulations often miss, specifically regarding hardware-software integration.

name: Yi Zhu
affiliation: MS student at UB's Robotics Program
work: Developed a 3D Gaussian Splatting-based digital twin of real-world environments to enable SIL testing in simulation
current: Software Engineer at iDrive Inc. contributing to Level 4 robotaxi systems (Control algorithms, MPC, and OCP solvers)
impact: Experience at CAVAS provided deep exposure to perception and control stacks, aligning research work with industry-standard autonomous vehicle development.
